steps.title: Set up the total weight expression
steps.content:
The truck carries three types of load: a 190-pound piece of equipment, \(x\) crates at 25 pounds each, and \(y\) crates at 62 pounds each.
$$\text{Total weight} = 190 + 25x + 62y$$

steps.title: Apply the maximum capacity constraint
steps.content:
The total weight cannot exceed 5,630 pounds:
$$190 + 25x + 62y \le 5{,}630$$
Subtract 190 from both sides:
$$25x + 62y \le 5{,}440$$

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• A: \(25x + 62y \le 5{,}440\) — matches our derived inequality exactly. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: \(25x + 62y \ge 5{,}440\) — the \(\ge\) direction would mean the load must be at least 5,440 pounds, not at most. Incorrect.
  • C: \(62x + 25y \le 5{,}630\) — the coefficients are swapped (62 with \(x\), 25 with \(y\)), and 190 was not subtracted. Incorrect.
  • D: \(62x + 25y \ge 5{,}630\) — both the coefficients are swapped and the inequality direction is wrong. Incorrect.
